originalTitle: DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict
title: DR Congo and Rwanda: Talking Problems at a Special Court
content:
Hello, wonderful kids! Guess what? Sometimes, even big countries have big problems, just like sometimes friends might have a misunderstanding. Today, we have some news from two amazing countries in Africa: the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short, and Rwanda. These two countries are neighbours, which means they live right next to each other, sharing a border, just like you might share a fence with your neighbour. They have had some tricky times and disagreements for a very, very long time. It's like a friendship where people have been arguing for many years. But now, something new is happening! DR Congo is going to a super special place called an "international court" to talk about these problems. They want to find a peaceful way to sort things out.

Two Neighbour Countries

Imagine Africa as a big, beautiful playground. In this playground, there are many different countries, like different teams or groups of friends. DR Congo is one of the biggest countries in Africa! It's super huge, with lots of big forests, beautiful rivers, and amazing animals like gorillas and okapis. **Did you know?** The Democratic Republic of Congo is so big that it's the second-largest country in Africa! It has a very famous river called the Congo River, which is the second-longest river in Africa. Next door to DR Congo is Rwanda. Rwanda is a much smaller country, but it's very famous for its beautiful green hills. People even call it the "Land of a Thousand Hills"! It's a country full of lovely green scenery and wonderful people. **Did you know?** Rwanda is known for its mountain gorillas! Many people come from all over the world to see these amazing animals living safely in their homes in the mountains.

What is This Special Court?

So, what is this "international court" we are talking about? It's like a very, very big school principal's office, but for countries! It's called the International Court of Justice, or ICJ for short. This court is not a place for fighting or shouting. Oh no! It's a place where countries can go to talk about their big problems and disagreements peacefully. It's like when you have a big problem with a friend, and you go to your teacher or parent to help you both talk it out and find a fair way to solve things. The court listens to both sides very carefully, just like a kind teacher would. The International Court of Justice is in a far-away city called The Hague, in a country named the Netherlands. But it's a place for the whole world! When countries can't agree, they can ask this court to help them understand each other and make things right. **Did you know?** The building where the International Court of Justice is located is called the Peace Palace! Isn't that a lovely name for a place where countries try to find peace?

Why are They Going to Court?

Now, let's talk about why DR Congo is taking Rwanda to this special court. DR Congo says that Rwanda has done some things that have caused problems and hurt them for a very, very long time. These problems didn't just start yesterday; they have been happening for many, many years. Think about if you had a friend who kept doing things that made you sad or caused trouble for your family. After a while, you would feel very upset, right? That's how DR Congo feels. They want the court to listen to their story and help them make things fair again. The problems they are talking about go back a very long time, even to a very sad and difficult period that happened in Rwanda in 1994. This was a terrible time in history, where many, many innocent people were hurt. It was a time of great sadness and pain for Rwanda.

A Sad Time in History: 1994

In 1994, something very, very sad happened in Rwanda. It was called the Rwanda genocide. This means that a large group of people were targeted and hurt very badly, and many lost their lives. It was an extremely dark and painful chapter for the people of Rwanda and for the whole world. After this very sad time, some people who had been part of the problems in Rwanda moved into DR Congo. This made things very complicated and led to even more problems and fighting in DR Congo. It was like a big wave of trouble that spread from one place to another. DR Congo says that because of these things, their country has suffered a lot. They want the court to recognize this and to make sure that countries respect each other and don't cause harm to their neighbours. They are asking the court to help them get justice. **Did you know?** Learning about history, even sad parts, helps us understand the world better and work towards making sure such things never happen again. It teaches us the importance of kindness and respecting everyone.

What Will Happen Next?

Now that DR Congo has gone to the court, the judges will start to listen. They will listen very carefully to what DR Congo says. Then, they will also listen to what Rwanda has to say. It's like two friends telling their side of the story to a teacher. It will take some time. These kinds of big country problems are not solved overnight. The court will look at all the information, all the facts, and try to understand everything. Their job is to help countries find a peaceful and fair way forward. Everyone hopes that this process at the International Court of Justice will help DR Congo and Rwanda find a way to live together peacefully as good neighbours. Peace means everyone can be safe, happy, and work together to make their countries even better.

Why This Matters for All of Us

Why is this news important for kids like you? Well, it shows us that even when big problems happen between countries, there are ways to try and solve them without fighting. It teaches us about talking, listening, and trying to find fair solutions. It's just like in your own life. If you have a problem with a friend, it's always better to talk about it with kindness and try to understand each other, maybe with the help of a grown-up, instead of just arguing. Countries are trying to do the same thing, but on a much bigger scale. We all want peace and friendship in our homes, in our schools, and in our countries. When countries talk things out at a court like this, it's a step towards making the world a more peaceful place for everyone, especially for children. **Did you know?** The word "justice" means fairness. When we talk about justice, we mean making sure that everyone is treated fairly and that wrongs are made right.

Hoping for Friendship

So, let's keep our fingers crossed and hope that DR Congo and Rwanda can use this special court to find a way to become good friends and neighbours again. When countries work together, amazing things can happen! They can share, help each other, and grow stronger. It's a big step towards healing and building a better future for all the wonderful people in both DR Congo and Rwanda. We hope for lots of sunshine and friendship for them both!
summary: DR Congo and Rwanda, two countries in Africa, are going to a special international court to talk about long-lasting problems and find a peaceful way to be good neighbours.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows countries can talk out their problems instead of fighting, helping everyone live in peace and friendship.

title: Tanzania suspends political rallies three years after lifting ban
content: The East African government says the move is necessary because of security threats.

originalTitle: Cape Verde looks to capitalise on World Cup heroics
title: Cape Verde's Big Football Surprise!
content:
Hello, super friends! I am your friendly news reporter, here to tell you something amazing from Africa! Today, we are going on a journey to a special place called Cape Verde. It's a country full of sunshine and big smiles!

Cape Verde: A Land of Islands!

Cape Verde is not just one big piece of land. Imagine many beautiful islands floating in the big, blue ocean! Yes, that's what an "archipelago" means. It's a country made up of lots of islands. Did you know? Cape Verde is in Africa! It's a wonderful part of our big continent. The people there love to dance and sing, and guess what else? They love football!

Kick, Pass, Score! That's Football!

Do you know football? It's a super fun game! Two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's net. When you get the ball in the net, it's called a "goal"! Everyone cheers loudly! You need to run a lot and work together. It's a game of friendship and teamwork. Boys and girls all over Africa love to play football. It makes us strong and happy!

The World Cup: A Very Big Party!

Now, imagine the biggest football game in the whole wide world! That's the World Cup! Teams from many, many countries play against each other. It only happens every four years. It's a very special time. It's like a big party where everyone watches the best players. To play in the World Cup is a very big dream for many footballers. It means your country is one of the best! Did you know? The World Cup is watched by billions of people! That's like almost everyone in the world watching together!

Cape Verde's Amazing Journey!

Guess what? A team from Cape Verde did something super amazing! They played so, so well in the big World Cup in 2026. They were a big surprise! No one thought they would do so well. They showed everyone how strong and talented they are. They played with all their hearts! This made everyone in Cape Verde so proud and happy. They were like superheroes on the field! Did you know? When a team is a "surprise package," it means they did much, much better than anyone expected! Like finding a hidden treasure!

Learning to Be Football Stars!

How did the Cape Verde players become so good? It's not magic! It's because they work very, very hard. They train every day. They run fast and learn new tricks with the ball. They have good coaches who teach them how to pass and score. The coaches help them become strong and smart players. Hard work and practice make you better at anything, even football!

Little Feet, Big Dreams!

The children in Cape Verde also love football very much. They play in the streets, on the beaches, and in the parks. They dream of being like their heroes who played in the World Cup. These young kids are the future stars! They are kicking the ball, laughing, and learning the game. Every kick is a step closer to their dreams. Did you know? Many famous footballers started playing when they were very, very young, just like you!

More Places to Play!

Now that Cape Verde has done so well, they want to help even more. They want to make more places for kids to play football. They want more green fields where children can run and kick the ball safely. This is called "developing footballing talent." It means helping more children become good at football. They are building new football schools and training grounds. This will help many children follow their dreams. Did you know? Having good places to play helps children learn faster and safer!

Our Heroes Inspire Us!

The players who played in the World Cup are like big brothers and sisters to the young kids. They show everyone that with hard work, you can achieve big dreams. They make the children believe in themselves. When you see someone from your country do something amazing, it makes you feel like you can do it too! These players are truly inspiring.

What Happens Next? More Football Fun!

Cape Verde wants to keep this amazing energy going. They want to use this happy feeling to make football even better for everyone. This is what "capitalise" means – using their success to build even more success! They want to find more talented kids. They want to train more good coaches. They want to make sure Cape Verde keeps shining bright in the world of football. They want to build a very strong foundation for football. Did you know? Building a strong "foundation" means making sure the very first steps are solid, so everything else can grow tall and strong!

Come On, Let's Play!

So, next time you kick a ball, remember Cape Verde! Remember their amazing story of hard work and big dreams. Maybe one day, you will be a football star too! Keep playing, keep dreaming, and keep smiling! Africa is full of amazing stories!
summary: Cape Verde, a small country of islands in Africa, surprised everyone by playing wonderfully in the 2026 World Cup, and now they want to help more children learn and love football.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how hard work can make big dreams come true, and it helps more children in Cape Verde play football and become stars.

title: US strikes on Iran test fragile ceasefire
content: The US has struck Iran for the first time since the two sides reached a Memorandum of Understanding on June 17.

originalTitle: Spain beat Uruguay 1-0 to clinch World Cup Group H top spot
title: Super Spain Wins Big Football Game!
content:
Hello, My Little Champions!

Hello, hello, hello, my amazing friends all across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some super exciting news from the big world of football. I hope you're all ready for some fun stories about heroes on the field! We have a fantastic game to tell you about today. Get ready to cheer!

Big News from the World Cup!

Guess what? There was a very important football match! Two big teams played against each other. One team was from a country called Spain, and the other team was from a country called Uruguay. It was a very close game, but in the end, Spain won! They scored one goal, and Uruguay didn't score any. So the score was 1-0 for Spain! Hooray for Spain!

What is the World Cup?

Did you know about the World Cup? It's like the biggest, most exciting football party on Earth! Teams from all over the world come together to play. They want to see which country has the very best football team. It only happens every four years, so it's a very special event. All the players try their hardest to win the beautiful golden trophy. It's a time when everyone watches football and cheers for their favorite teams. It's truly magical!

Spain vs. Uruguay: Who Played?

In this special game, Spain wore their bright jerseys, and Uruguay wore theirs. Both teams had super strong players who ran very fast and kicked the ball with all their might. They wanted to win very much. They passed the ball, they tackled gently, and they tried to score goals. The crowd in the stadium was very loud, cheering for every kick! It was a very exciting match to watch.

The Super Goal!

How did Spain score their one goal? Well, a player from Spain kicked the ball towards Uruguay's goal. The ball went really fast! The goalkeeper from Uruguay tried his best to stop it. He's a very good goalkeeper, but sometimes, the ball just finds its way in! It was a quick moment, and then, *whoosh!* The ball was in the net! Everyone from Spain and their fans jumped up and cheered so loudly. It was the only goal of the whole game, and it made all the difference. That one goal was enough for Spain to win!

What Does This Mean for Spain?

Because Spain won this game, they did something very important. They won their 'group'! You can think of a group like a small mini-competition inside the big World Cup. Teams play other teams in their group first. By winning their group, Spain showed everyone that they are strong and ready to keep playing. It means they get to stay in the World Cup and play even more games! They are moving forward to try and win the big trophy. They are very happy and proud.

What About Uruguay?

Uruguay is a very good team, and they have won the World Cup before, a long time ago! So they are called 'champions'. They played with all their heart in this game, but sometimes in football, one team has to go home. Because they lost this game, Uruguay's journey in this World Cup has come to an end. We say 'well played' to Uruguay for trying their best! It's sad for them, but they can be proud of how hard they tried. They will come back another time.

Why We Love Football!

Football is so amazing because it brings people together. Kids like you and me, and grown-ups too, all watch and cheer. We learn about teamwork because all the players work together to win. We learn about being fair and playing nicely. And it's so much fun to watch the ball fly and see who can score! Did you know that football is played in almost every country in the world? It truly is a global game!

Amazing African Football Fun!

Here in Africa, we LOVE football! We have so many wonderful football players who play for teams all around the world. Kids play football everywhere – in the streets, in fields, and in schools. We cheer so loudly for our favorite teams. Football makes us feel excited and proud. Did you know that some of the fastest and strongest players in the world come from Africa? We are super proud of our African football stars!

Keep Cheering!

So, Spain is still playing in the World Cup, and Uruguay is going home. That's the news from the big game! Keep watching, keep cheering for your favorite teams, and remember that football is all about fun, friendship, and trying your best! See you next time for more exciting news!
summary: Spain beat Uruguay 1-0 in a big World Cup game, which means Spain gets to play more games and Uruguay will go home after trying their best.
whyMatters: This is important because Spain won their group, showing they are a strong team, and they get to keep playing in the exciting World Cup to try and win the big trophy!

originalTitle: US conducts strikes on Iran after attack on cargo ship
title: News from Far Away: Big Boats, Flying Robots, and Peace!
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends!

Welcome, welcome, wonderful explorers! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you stories from all over our incredible planet Earth. Even though we are here in beautiful Africa, sometimes things happen very far away that are important for us to know about too. Today, we have a story about keeping things safe, especially on the big, big ocean where giant boats travel. It's about two countries, the USA and Iran, and what happened when a very important kind of boat was in trouble.

What Are We Talking About?

Imagine our world has many big neighbourhoods, and sometimes, grown-ups in these neighbourhoods have disagreements. This story is about something that happened between the USA and Iran, two countries quite far from us, but still part of our global family. It’s about making sure that the big pathways on the sea, like roads for boats, stay safe for everyone. Because when the seas are safe, all good things can keep moving around the world to reach us!

Boats That Carry Treasure!

Have you ever seen a really, really big boat? I mean, a boat so huge it looks like a floating city! These are called "cargo ships." They are like giant trucks, but they travel on water! These amazing ships carry almost everything you can think of! From the clothes you wear, to the toys you play with, to the food we eat, and even the fuel for our cars – so many things travel on these big cargo ships. They sail across huge oceans, from one country to another, bringing important goods to people everywhere, including here in Africa. They are like busy, hardworking giants of the sea. Did you know? Over 80% of all the goods we use every day travel on these big cargo ships across the oceans!

A Little Problem on the Sea

One day, a very sad thing happened. One of these important cargo ships, carrying valuable things for people, was attacked. "Attacked" means someone tried to hurt it or stop it from going where it needed to go. This happened in a place called the Middle East, which is far away from us, near some very important seas where many cargo ships travel. This made traveling on those seas scary and dangerous. When a cargo ship is attacked, it means that the things it carries might not get to the people who need them. It can make things difficult for everyone, like when a road is closed and cars can't get through.

Meet the USA's Helpers

Now, the USA has a group of people, like a big team of helpers, called "US Central Command." Think of them like a special police team or a helpful group of grown-ups who work together to keep things safe and peaceful in different parts of the world. When this team heard about the attacked cargo ship and how unsafe the seas were becoming, they felt it was important to do something to help. They wanted to make sure other ships could travel safely again. They decided to take action to stop the people who were causing trouble on the sea. Their job is to protect people and make sure everyone can live and travel safely.

What Did the USA Do?

To help make the seas safe again, the USA decided to do something. They used special airplanes and other tools to hit certain places. When we say they "struck" places, it means they aimed at specific spots. They did this to stop the bad things from happening again. It's like when a grown-up sees a bully on the playground and steps in to make sure everyone can play nicely and safely. They did not just hit any random place. They hit very specific places that were being used to store special flying objects that could cause trouble. They wanted to make it harder for the attackers to hurt more ships.

What Are Missiles and Drones?

Let's talk about the special flying objects. The USA hit places where "missiles" and "drones" were kept. These words might sound tricky, but let's make them simple. A "missile" is like a super-fast, powerful dart or rocket. It can fly very quickly and far, reaching a specific target. These are powerful tools that countries sometimes have. And a "drone"? Imagine a remote-control airplane or a flying robot! Some drones are small, like toys, but some are very big and can fly for a long time. They can see things from high up, or even carry things. Did you know? Drones are also used for many good things, like delivering medicine to faraway places, helping farmers check their crops, or even filming amazing nature videos!

Where Were They Stored?

The USA specifically hit places that are called "storage facilities." Think of a storage facility like a very big garage or a giant toy box. It's a place where things are kept when they are not being used. So, the USA hit places where these missiles and drones were kept by Iran. They also hit "coastal radar positions." "Coastal" means right next to the sea. "Radar" is like a big ear and eye for ships and planes. Radar helps machines see and hear what's coming, even when it's dark, rainy, or foggy. These radar stations help watch over the sea. The USA wanted to stop them from being used for bad things, like helping to attack ships.

Why Did This Happen?

So, why did the USA do this? It's like when there's a problem in our homes or schools. Sometimes, people disagree, or someone does something that hurts others. When the cargo ship was attacked, it made the sea dangerous for everyone. The USA said they took action to stop more attacks from happening and to help keep the important sea routes safe for all ships to pass. They want to make sure that the big ocean roads stay open and safe, so that food, medicine, and all the things we need can keep moving around the world without trouble. It's all about trying to bring back peace and safety.

Far Away, But We Are All Connected

This news might come from far away, from a part of the world called the Middle East. But remember, our planet Earth is like one big neighbourhood, and we are all connected. When things are not peaceful or safe in one part of the world, it can sometimes affect other parts too. For example, if it's harder for cargo ships to travel, it can sometimes make things like fuel or yummy food more expensive for everyone, even for us here. Did you know? All the oceans on Earth are connected, just like all the people are connected in many ways!

What Can We Hope For?

Even when difficult things happen, we must always hope for peace. Many, many people around the world, and all of us here, want all countries to be friends and work together. It's so important for people to talk and share their ideas to solve problems without fighting. Just like we learn to share and be kind in school, countries need to learn to share our world peacefully. Let's hope that the leaders of these countries will find good ways to talk to each other, to keep the seas safe, and to make our beautiful world a peaceful place for all children and grown-ups.

Our World Needs Kindness

Remember, every little act of kindness and every peaceful word helps. We can all do our part by being kind to our friends, neighbours, and everyone around us. Because peace starts with each one of us. Just imagine a world where everyone helps each other and lives happily together. That's the dream we all share, and it's something we should always work towards. Let's send our hopes for peace to everyone! Did you know? Talking about your problems calmly is almost always the best way to solve them, no matter if you're a child or a country's leader!
summary: After a big cargo ship was attacked on the sea, the USA used special planes to hit some places in Iran that stored fast rockets and flying robots, aiming to make the important sea routes safe again.
whyMatters: This is important because it's about keeping big ships safe so they can deliver food, toys, and other important things to everyone, and it shows that countries are working to stop fighting and find ways to live in peace.
